diagrams.title: Symmetric Encryption Process
diagrams.description: Shows the basic encryption and decryption flow in symmetric cryptography
elements: Sender and Receiver both have the same secret key

diagrams.title: ECB vs CBC Mode Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of how ECB and CBC process plaintext blocks
elements: ECB: Plaintext blocks → [独立加密] → Ciphertext blocks (each separate)
CBC: Plaintext + Previous Ciphertext → [XOR] → [加密] → New Ciphertext (chained)
IV shown as first input in CBC
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why CBC is more secure than ECB

diagrams.title: AES Structure Overview
diagrams.description: Shows AES algorithm structure with rounds
elements: Input (128-bit block) → Initial Round (AddRoundKey) → 9/11/13 Main Rounds (SubBytes, ShiftRows, MixColumns, AddRoundKey) → Final Round → Output
Each round: SubBytes (substitution), ShiftRows (permutation), MixColumns (mixing), AddRoundKey (key mixing)

diagrams.title: Key Distribution Problem
diagrams.description: Shows the challenge of sharing symmetric keys between multiple users
elements: User A needs unique key with User B, C, D, E...
Visual showing complexity increases with users
diagrams.purpose: Understanding scalability issues with symmetric encryption
